roadmap.note: Bug-fix feature with a ratified DESIGN wave (feature-delta.md + ADR-0061) but NO DISTILL wave. There are NO .feature files and NO distill/test-scenarios.md, so test_file and scenario_name on every step are ARCHITECT-PROPOSED, not sourced from existing acceptance files. The crafter creates the named test file(s) during PREPARE/RED. Reuse Analysis (feature-delta § 5) is already done: exactly ONE CREATE NEW (the veth provisioner, step 01-02); every other surface is EXTEND/REUSE. DES contract is the legacy 5-phase (PREPARE, RED_ACCEPTANCE, RED_UNIT, GREEN, COMMIT); steps are crafted Outside-In TDD. No new GitHub issues — DQ-2 (#194) and DQ-3 (#195) are already created and cited in the ADR; this fix is IPv4-only and ships implicit-by-default veth names, both already tracked. Mutation gate is per-feature (>=80% kill) and applies to the EBUSY-classification logic (01-01) and the pure gateway/route-derivation fn (01-02); the real `ip` shell-out and the serve-boot wiring are Tier-3-covered and excluded from the nextest mutants lane, mirroring the existing overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs attach code.

phases.name: Single-node veth dataplane wiring (ADR-0061)
phases.description: Fix the production single-node `overdrive serve` default-config boot abort (EBUSY: two XDP programs both attaching to `lo`) and the deeper `lo`/generic-XDP cloned-skb correctness gap. Per ADR-0061: honest EBUSY diagnostic, a new host-netns veth provisioner, a veth-named config default, serve-boot wiring before EbpfDataplane::new, and a Tier-3 regression test. Walking-skeleton-first order: diagnostic (01-01) makes the failure honest, provisioner (01-02) is the one CREATE NEW, config+wiring (01-03) removes the bug-shaped default and auto-provisions at boot, Tier-3 (01-04) proves two distinct programs attach to two distinct veth ifaces and serve boots.

steps.name: Honest EBUSY diagnostic — IfaceXdpSlotBusy variant + EBUSY classification (ADR-0061 § 5 / D3)
steps.description: Add the typed diagnostic the masking `DRV_MODE` string hides, per ADR-0061 § 5 and feature-delta § 7. (1) In `overdrive-core`, add `DataplaneError::IfaceXdpSlotBusy { iface: String }` to the `DataplaneError` enum (crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/dataplane.rs, the enum at L19). `#[error(...)]` Display names the iface AND the real cause AND the remediation: "interface `<iface>` already has an XDP program attached (EBUSY); another program owns its single XDP hook. Single-node default expects a dedicated veth pair — verify client_iface != backend_iface and that no stale Overdrive XDP program is attached (`ip link show <iface>`; detach per debugging.md § Leftover XDP attachments across runs)." Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Label enums own their string representation" / § Errors (distinct failure modes get distinct error variants). (2) In `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs`, the two `AttachOutcome::Propagate(e)` arms (forward L505-509 attaching xdp_service_map_lookup to client_iface; reverse L550-554 attaching xdp_reverse_nat_lookup to backend_iface) currently return `DataplaneError::LoadFailed(format!("...attach({iface}, DRV_MODE): {e}"))`, masking EBUSY behind a misleading native-attach string. Classify `raw_os_error() == libc::EBUSY` into the new `IfaceXdpSlotBusy { iface }` variant BEFORE falling through to the existing `LoadFailed`/`DRV_MODE` string. The classification mirrors the existing `should_fallback_to_generic` / `classify_attach_result` precedent in the same file (which inspects `ProgramError::SyscallError(SyscallError { io_error, .. })` for `EOPNOTSUPP`/`ENOTSUP`) — reuse the same unwrap shape to reach `raw_os_error()`. Extract the EBUSY check into a small pure classifier helper next to `should_fallback_to_generic` so both Propagate arms call it with their respective iface name. (3) The new variant bubbles up unchanged through the existing `DataplaneBootError::Construct` (ADR-0052 § 3) — NO new boot-error variant; the Construct path already carries `client_iface`/`backend_iface`. No attach-shape change; no behavioral change on the happy path. This is the walking skeleton: it makes the EBUSY failure honest before any of the wiring that makes EBUSY unreachable on the default path lands.
steps.criteria: `DataplaneError::IfaceXdpSlotBusy { iface: String }` exists in crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/dataplane.rs with `#[error(...)]` Display naming the iface, the EBUSY cause, and the `ip link show` / detach remediation. Both `AttachOutcome::Propagate(e)` arms in crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs classify `raw_os_error() == libc::EBUSY` into `IfaceXdpSlotBusy { iface: <that arm's iface>.to_owned() }` BEFORE the existing `LoadFailed(...DRV_MODE...)` fallthrough. A pure classifier helper (sibling to `should_fallback_to_generic`) maps a synthetic attach `Err` to `Some(iface)` on EBUSY and `None` otherwise. Default-lane unit tests (against synthetic `SyscallError`/`ProgramError`-shaped values, exactly like the existing fallback classifier tests — no real attach) assert: (a) an EBUSY `io_error` classifies to `IfaceXdpSlotBusy` carrying the correct iface string; (b) a non-EBUSY `io_error` (e.g. EPERM, ENODEV) does NOT classify to `IfaceXdpSlotBusy` and falls through to `LoadFailed`; (c) the Display string of `IfaceXdpSlotBusy { iface }` contains the iface name and the substring `EBUSY` and a `client_iface != backend_iface` remediation hint. `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --features integration-tests` clean; `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` clean. Per-feature mutation gate (>=80% kill) covers the new classifier helper and its branch.
steps.test_file: crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs (inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests, sibling to the existing should_fallback_to_generic tests)
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: ADR-0061 § 5 — EBUSY attach failure surfaces as IfaceXdpSlotBusy, not a masking DRV_MODE LoadFailed string (unit-level, synthetic SyscallError) | Unit tests: classify_ebusy_attach_err_yields_iface_xdp_slot_busy, classify_non_ebusy_attach_err_falls_through_to_load_failed, iface_xdp_slot_busy_display_names_iface_and_ebusy_and_remediation
steps.implementation_notes: EXTEND only — no new component. The classifier helper is pure and default-lane unit-testable against synthetic `aya::programs::ProgramError::SyscallError(SyscallError { call, io_error })` values; do NOT attempt a real EBUSY attach in a unit test (that is the Tier-3 surface in 01-04). Reach `raw_os_error()` exactly as the existing `should_fallback_to_generic` reaches it — same `matches!` / destructure shape, swapping `EOPNOTSUPP | ENOTSUP` for `EBUSY`. Use `libc::EBUSY` (not a hardcoded 16). Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § Errors do NOT collapse EBUSY into the existing `LoadFailed(String)` — that is the exact catch-all-prescribes-wrong-remediation anti-pattern the variant exists to fix. The variant is `#[error("...")]` via thiserror; no manual `Display` impl. This step closes no wiring — EBUSY is still reachable on the default `lo`/`lo` path until 01-03 lands; that is intentional walking-skeleton ordering. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: the classifier is an exact-branch error-classification fn (EBUSY -> variant; else -> fallthrough); express as classic example-based unit tests over the enumerated `raw_os_error()` cases (EBUSY, EPERM, ENODEV, None) rather than a property — the input domain is a small fixed set of errno discriminants, not a quantifiable universe. Compensate with the per-feature mutation gate, which is the real kill signal on the branch.
acceptance_scenarios: ADR-0061 § 5 — EBUSY attach failure surfaces as IfaceXdpSlotBusy not a masking DRV_MODE LoadFailed string (unit-level)

steps.name: Veth provisioner (CREATE NEW, adapter-host) — pure gateway/route derivation + idempotent ip shell-out (ADR-0061 § 3)
steps.description: The single CREATE NEW of this feature (feature-delta § 5 Reuse Analysis). A new production `adapter-host` component that stands up the single-node veth pair in the HOST netns — NO netns machinery — per ADR-0061 § 3. It lives in `overdrive-control-plane` (the crate where the serve-boot composition consumes `DataplaneConfig` and calls `EbpfDataplane::new_with_pin_dir` — confirmed at crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs L1024-1067; the ADR says "overdrive-worker or overdrive-host" but the boot-wiring SSOT is here, so the provisioner is co-located with its only caller). It MUST NOT live in `overdrive-testing` (dev-dep-only, shells `ip netns add`; a production binary must never link it — `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Shared real-infra test fixtures"). It mirrors a SUBSET of crates/overdrive-testing/src/netns.rs::ThreeIfaceTopology::create (the `ip link add <a> type veth peer name <b>` / `ip addr add` / `ip link set up` / `ip route add` sequence) WITHOUT the `ip netns add` parts. Split into two surfaces: (A) a PURE default-lane-unit-testable derivation fn `derive_veth_plan(client_iface: &str, backend_iface: &str, vip_range: &Ipv4Net) -> VethProvisionPlan` that computes the on-link gateway IP for `ovd-veth-cli` and the `ip route add <vip_range> dev ovd-veth-cli` from the first `[dataplane.vip_allocator].ranges` entry (ADR-0049) — PERSIST INPUTS NOT DERIVED STATE (`.claude/rules/development.md`): the plan is derived at provision time from the range, never persisted. First usable host address of the range -> gateway on `ovd-veth-cli`; the backend-side gateway derives from a backend range (or a documented offset of the same range for Phase-1 single-range configs — crafter picks the smallest honest derivation and documents it). (B) an async `provision(plan: &VethProvisionPlan) -> Result<(), VethProvisionError>` that performs the real `ip` shell-out, gated behind the crate's `integration-tests` feature and `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]`. provision() is IDEMPOTENT detect-and-reuse per ADR-0061 § 3.1: `ip link show ovd-veth-cli` FIRST; if the pair pre-exists (created by a prior serve boot, an OS image, or a Lima/Yocto provisioner) ADOPT it untouched — do not recreate, do not fail; a pre-existing route is left in place. Only `ip link add ... type veth peer name ...` + assign addresses + bring up + `ip route add` when the pair is ABSENT. Never tear down on shutdown (DQ-4 leave-and-reuse). Typed `VethProvisionError` (thiserror) with distinct variants per failure mode (link-show-failed, link-add-failed, addr-add-failed, route-add-failed) per § Errors — do NOT collapse to one String variant.
steps.criteria: New module (e.g. crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/veth_provisioner.rs) exists, `adapter-host`-class (lives in overdrive-control-plane, NOT overdrive-testing). `derive_veth_plan(client_iface, backend_iface, &Ipv4Net) -> VethProvisionPlan` is a PURE fn with NO I/O — default-lane unit tests assert: (a) gateway IP for ovd-veth-cli is the first usable host address of the first vip range (e.g. 10.96.0.0/24 -> 10.96.0.1); (b) the derived route is `<vip_range> dev ovd-veth-cli`; (c) the plan carries the literal iface names from config (ovd-veth-cli / ovd-veth-bk by default), not hardcoded; (d) derivation is deterministic (same inputs -> same plan across N invocations). `provision(&plan)` is async, `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]`, and gated behind the `integration-tests` feature (its real `ip` shell-out is NOT in the default lane). `VethProvisionError` is a thiserror enum with distinct variants per `ip` failure mode (no single catch-all String). The idempotent detect-and-reuse path is structurally present (link-show before link-add; adopt-on-present). `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --features integration-tests` clean; clippy -D warnings clean. Per-feature mutation gate (>=80% kill) covers `derive_veth_plan` (the pure derivation). The real `ip` shell-out in `provision` is excluded from the nextest mutants lane (Tier-3-covered in 01-04), mirroring the existing overdrive-dataplane attach code's exclusion.
steps.test_file: crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/veth_provisioner.rs (inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests for derive_veth_plan) + crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/veth_provision_idempotent.rs (Tier-3 shell-out, integration-tests-gated)
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: ADR-0061 § 3 — provisioner derives on-link gateway + route from the first vip_allocator range and stands up the host-netns veth pair idempotently | Unit tests: derive_veth_plan_first_usable_host_is_gateway, derive_veth_plan_route_targets_client_iface, derive_veth_plan_uses_config_iface_names, derive_veth_plan_deterministic | Tier-3: provision_creates_pair_when_absent, provision_adopts_preexisting_pair_without_recreating
steps.implementation_notes: CREATE NEW — the ONLY new component in this feature (feature-delta § 5). Read crates/overdrive-testing/src/netns.rs::ThreeIfaceTopology::create for the `ip` command sequence and the address/route ordering, then strip every `ip netns add` / `ip link set <if> netns <ns>` line — single-node runs entirely in the host netns. Do NOT take a dependency on overdrive-testing (dev-dep-only); copy the host-netns-relevant subset into the new production module. The Ipv4Net type is already in the workspace graph (vip_allocator_config.rs uses `ipnet::Ipv4Net`); reuse it. Backend-side gateway for ovd-veth-bk: Phase-1 single-range configs derive it from a documented offset or a second range when present — pick the smallest honest rule and rustdoc it; the e2e correctness of the steering is proven in 01-04, this step proves derivation + idempotent provision. CAP_NET_ADMIN is already a precondition (XDP attach + cgroup) so provision() adds no new privilege. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): unit tests for `derive_veth_plan` SHOULD be property-based where the contract supports it — e.g. for any valid /24..../30 vip range, the derived gateway is within the range and equals first-usable-host, and the route CIDR equals the input range — use a generated-range strategy + an invariant assertion. Single-example cases (the 10.96.0.0/24 -> 10.96.0.1 golden) are acceptable as readable anchors alongside the property. The Tier-3 provision tests REMAIN single-example (they verify real-`ip` wiring + idempotent adopt, not an invariant) and run only via `cargo xtask lima run --`.
acceptance_scenarios: ADR-0061 § 3 — provisioner derives gateway + route from first vip range and stands up host-netns veth pair idempotently

steps.name: Veth-named config default + serve-boot provisioning before EbpfDataplane::new (ADR-0061 § 1 / § 3)
steps.description: Remove the bug-shaped default and wire the provisioner into the boot composition. (1) Config default change per ADR-0061 § 1 and feature-delta § 5: replace `DataplaneConfig::loopback()` (crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dataplane_config.rs L61-63, which sets BOTH ifaces to `lo` — the origin of the bug) with a veth-named default helper (e.g. `DataplaneConfig::single_node_veth()` returning `client_iface = "ovd-veth-cli"`, `backend_iface = "ovd-veth-bk"`). The wiring site is the default `ServerConfig.dataplane` at crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs L526 (`dataplane: Some(DataplaneConfig::loopback())`). Single-cut per project policy: confirm every `loopback()` consumer via grep (lib.rs L526 default; lib.rs L381 docstring; convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot.rs L138 test fixture) and migrate them. KEEP `loopback()` ONLY if a pure non-attach test fixture genuinely needs `lo` (it never attaches) — otherwise delete-and-replace, do not gate (`.claude/rules/development.md` § Deletion discipline / feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md). The production parser `parse_dataplane_section` is UNCHANGED (it already requires both fields, ADR-0052 § 3) — only the default value the test/boot fixtures carry changes. (2) Serve-boot wiring per ADR-0061 § 3 and feature-delta § 8: in the production branch of the boot composition (crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs L1031-1067, the `else` arm taken when `dataplane_override` is None), call the provisioner BEFORE `EbpfDataplane::new_with_pin_dir` (L1042-1062). Extend the existing ADR-0052 parse -> construct -> probe -> use sequence ADDITIVELY (provision -> construct -> probe -> use); do NOT reshape it. Resolve the vip range from `[dataplane.vip_allocator].ranges` (already parsed via vip_allocator_config), call `derive_veth_plan(client_iface, backend_iface, &range)` then `provision(&plan).await`. Provision only fires for the production (non-override) path; tests injecting SimDataplane via `dataplane_override` skip it entirely. Map a `VethProvisionError` into the boot-error surface via a dedicated `#[from]` variant on `DataplaneBootError` (NOT `.map_err(|e| ...internal(...))` — `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Never flatten a typed error to Internal(String)"); surface refusal as a structured `health.startup.refused` event mirroring the existing probe-refusal arm at L1105-1117.
steps.criteria: `DataplaneConfig::loopback()` is replaced by a veth-named default helper returning client_iface="ovd-veth-cli", backend_iface="ovd-veth-bk"; the default `ServerConfig.dataplane` at lib.rs L526 uses it. Every prior `loopback()` consumer is migrated (grep for `loopback()` in crates/overdrive-control-plane returns only an intentional pure-non-attach fixture, if any, with a rustdoc note — otherwise zero). The production boot branch (dataplane_override None) calls `derive_veth_plan(...)` + `provision(&plan).await` BEFORE `EbpfDataplane::new_with_pin_dir`; the ADR-0052 parse->construct->probe->use order is preserved (provision slots before construct, additively). A `VethProvisionError` maps into `DataplaneBootError` via a dedicated `#[from]` variant (no `internal(String)` flattening); provision failure emits a `health.startup.refused` event and refuses to boot. The SimDataplane-override path (test fixtures) does NOT call the provisioner. Two-NIC override path is NOT regressed — when an operator names real ifaces the existing boot resolution is unchanged (the provisioner only fires for the default veth path / production branch; the override branch is untouched). `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --features integration-tests` clean; clippy -D warnings clean. Default-lane unit test asserts the new default helper returns the two veth names (and they differ — client_iface != backend_iface, the property that makes EBUSY structurally unreachable).
steps.test_file: crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dataplane_config.rs (inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests for the default helper) + crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/serve_boot_provisions_veth.rs (Tier-3 boot-wiring, integration-tests-gated; may co-locate with 01-04's regression test)
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: ADR-0061 § 1 — single-node default config carries two distinct veth ifaces (not lo/lo); ADR-0061 § 3 — serve boot provisions the veth pair before EbpfDataplane::new | Unit tests: single_node_veth_default_returns_distinct_veth_names, single_node_veth_default_client_ne_backend
steps.implementation_notes: Depends on 01-02 (needs `derive_veth_plan` + `provision` to call). Single-cut: do NOT leave a deprecated `loopback()` behind unless a pure non-attach fixture (one that never reaches the XDP attach path) genuinely needs `lo` — verify by reading each of the 3 call sites (lib.rs L526, lib.rs L381 docstring, convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot.rs L138). If convergence_loop test injects SimDataplane via dataplane_override it never attaches, so it MAY keep a renamed loopback-shaped helper OR adopt the veth-named default (crafter picks the smallest diff that keeps that test honest). The provisioner call is in the production `else` branch ONLY (override branch = tests, skips provision). Preserve the L1041 `#[allow(unused_mut)]` and the L1089 integration-tests-gated probe-fault seam — insert provision ABOVE the `new_with_pin_dir` map_or_else, not inside it. The `DataplaneBootError` `#[from]` variant for `VethProvisionError` follows the existing `Construct`/`Probe` variant precedent in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/error.rs. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: the default-helper unit test is an exact-value assertion (returns the two literal veth names) — example-based is correct; the wiring itself is integration-shaped (verified single-example at Tier-3 in 01-04), not an invariant. The mutation gate does not cover the serve-boot wiring (Tier-3-covered, excluded from nextest mutants lane like the existing attach code).
acceptance_scenarios: ADR-0061 § 1 — single-node default config carries two distinct veth ifaces not lo/lo
ADR-0061 § 3 — serve boot provisions the veth pair before EbpfDataplane::new

steps.name: Tier-3 regression — two XDP programs attach to two veth ifaces; serve boots; IfaceXdpSlotBusy fires on single-iface collision (ADR-0061 § 8 hard-constraint)
steps.description: The real-veth regression test that proves the fix end-to-end, per feature-delta § 8 hard-constraints. Two `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]`, `integration-tests`-gated tests run via `cargo xtask lima run --` (Linux/Lima only): (1) HAPPY PATH — with the default single-node config (ovd-veth-cli / ovd-veth-bk), the provisioner stands up the pair, the two distinct XDP programs (xdp_service_map_lookup on the client side, xdp_reverse_nat_lookup on the backend side) BOTH attach to their two distinct veth ifaces, and serve boots: the EBUSY no longer fires on the default path. Assert via observable kernel state (per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § Tier-3 assertion rules — assert on kernel side effects, never program-internal reachability): both ifaces carry an attached XDP program (`ip link show` / `bpftool prog show` showing two distinct xdp programs on two distinct ifindexes), and the boot composition reaches the post-probe ready state without a `DataplaneBootError`. (2) DIAGNOSTIC — when both ifaces are pointed at ONE real iface (the residual operator-error case ADR-0061 § 5 guards), the second attach returns EBUSY and the boot error is `IfaceXdpSlotBusy { iface }` (NOT a masking DRV_MODE `LoadFailed`); assert by matching the typed variant on the returned `DataplaneBootError::Construct { source: DataplaneError::IfaceXdpSlotBusy { .. }, .. }`. Reuse the ThreeIfaceTopology / veth fixture shape from overdrive-testing as the test-side topology helper (dev-dep, allowed in tests). MUST NOT regress crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/reverse_nat_e2e.rs (ADR-0061 § 8 / Consequences — Option E touches no kernel-side program, so reverse_nat_e2e is untouched; this step adds new tests, it does not edit that file).
steps.criteria: A new Tier-3 integration test (crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/serve_boot_provisions_veth.rs, integration-tests-gated, #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]) proves: (1) HAPPY — with the default veth config, the provisioner creates/adopts the pair AND both xdp_service_map_lookup and xdp_reverse_nat_lookup attach to two distinct veth ifaces AND the boot composition completes past the dataplane probe with no DataplaneBootError; observable kernel state (XDP program present on each of the two ifindexes) is asserted, not program-internal branch reachability. (2) DIAGNOSTIC — pointing both ifaces at one real iface yields a `DataplaneBootError::Construct` whose `source` is `DataplaneError::IfaceXdpSlotBusy { iface }` (typed-variant match, not a Display grep), proving 01-01's classification fires on a real EBUSY. The existing reverse_nat_e2e.rs Tier-3 test is UNCHANGED and still passes (run it via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --features integration-tests` to confirm no regression). Tests run via `cargo xtask lima run --` (cgroup/XDP/real-kernel surface); the macOS `--no-run` compile gate passes too. Leftover-XDP hygiene per debugging.md § "Leftover XDP attachments across runs" is respected (RAII detach or documented cleanup in the fixture).
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: ADR-0061 § 8 — default-config serve boots with two XDP programs on two distinct veth ifaces (no EBUSY); ADR-0061 § 5 — both-ifaces-one-iface collision surfaces IfaceXdpSlotBusy | Tier-3 tests: serve_boot_default_veth_attaches_two_programs_and_boots, both_ifaces_one_real_iface_yields_iface_xdp_slot_busy
steps.implementation_notes: Depends on 01-01 (the IfaceXdpSlotBusy variant + classification the diagnostic test matches on) AND 01-03 (the provisioner wiring + veth default the happy-path test exercises). Tier-3 only: assert on observable kernel side effects per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § Tier-3 assertion rules — `ip link show <iface>` / `bpftool prog show` for the two-distinct-program assertion; the typed `DataplaneBootError`/`DataplaneError` variant for the diagnostic assertion. Reuse the overdrive-testing veth/topology fixture (dev-dependency, allowed in tests — NOT in production). Run via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'binary(integration)'`. Before re-running, sweep leftover XDP per debugging.md § "Leftover XDP attachments across runs" (the canonical detach one-liner) — a stale XDP program on lo/veth from a prior crashed run produces false EBUSY/hang signatures. Do NOT edit reverse_nat_e2e.rs — confirm it still passes as the no-regression check (ADR-0061 § 8). This step is the only one with no production `files_to_modify` beyond the test entrypoint wiring — it is a Tier-3 regression test, the explicit hard-constraint deliverable, not a validation-only step (it creates a real test file with two executed scenarios). EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: integration/E2E tests verify wiring, not invariants — single-example is correct per the standing mandate.
acceptance_scenarios: ADR-0061 § 8 — default-config serve boots with two XDP programs on two distinct veth ifaces (no EBUSY)
